story: Mom drops us at Grandma's and drives off. We eat sugared strawberries and color; I close my eyes as the radio hums. On her way to work, Mom phones Grandma: she'll swing back at noon to check on us. Later, Owen rides the rocking horse, slips, and wails. Grandma snatches him up, scolding me. From the kitchen she calls, "Snack!" Head aching, I stand in the hall thinking: I don't want a snack. I want to go home.
implausible_knowledge: On her way to work, Mom phones Grandma: she'll swing back at noon to check on us.
question: Right after Grandma calls "Snack!" and Isabelle stands in the hall wishing she could go home, what will she most likely do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Wait by the front window for Mom to arrive soon.
answer_without_knowledge: Linger in the hallway and avoid going to the kitchen for the snack.
